title: USD Debasement & Hard Asset Premium
description: The structural expansion of the Fed balance sheet, record US deficit spending (>6% GDP), and de-dollarization pressures from BRICS+ create a long-term tailwind for hard assets — gold, silver, commodities, and energy — as dollar purchasing power erodes.

conviction_entries.note: Initiating thesis. Fed balance sheet at $7.7T, deficit widening.
Gold breaks $2,100 ATH. Central bank buying confirmed at 1,037 tonnes.
Gold $2,650. Fed cutting. China and India aggressive gold accumulation.
Trump tariffs create dollar strength headwind short-term.

bets.notes: Core hard asset position.
Leveraged play on gold via miners.
Copper = real economy debasement hedge + AI demand overlap.

title: AI Infrastructure Supercycle
description: The buildout of AI training and inference capacity represents the largest CapEx cycle since the 1990s internet boom. Hyperscalers are committing $300B+ annually in data center spend through 2026, creating compounding demand across semiconductors, power, cooling, and copper.

conviction_entries.note: GPT-4 moment clear. Hyperscaler capex guidance blowing out.
NVDA Q3: revenue $18.1B vs $16B est. Data center +400% YoY.
NVDA Q1 FY25: $26B revenue. Blackwell ahead of schedule.
DeepSeek concerns — open source efficiency improving faster.
DeepSeek R1: inference efficiency 10x better. Jevons paradox?

bets.notes: Diversified semiconductor exposure.
AI power demand play. Nuclear + nat gas fleet.
Blackwell supercycle. Small size due to concentration risk.

title: Yield Curve Re-steepening
description: As the Fed cuts short rates while long-end inflation expectations stay elevated, the curve re-steepens to +50-150bps, creating a structural tailwind for bank net interest margins.

conviction_entries.note: Fed starting to signal cuts. Curve still deeply inverted.
Fed cuts 50bps. 10Y-2Y spread moves from -50 to -20bps.
Curve now +10bps. KRE up 18%. Fed signaling slower cuts.
Strong jobs pushing back cut timeline. Bear steepener risk.

bets.notes: Direct NIM expansion play.
Short long bonds via TBF.

title: Electricity Bottleneck & Power Infrastructure
description: AI data centers, EV adoption, and electrification of everything is creating massive electricity demand that existing grid infrastructure cannot support. This bottleneck drives investment into solar, nuclear, grid modernization, and energy storage.

conviction_entries.note: Initiating thesis. Data center power demand projections rising sharply. Grid permitting backlog growing.
Microsoft-Constellation nuclear deal signals Big Tech taking power scarcity seriously.
FERC grid interconnection queue hits 2,600 GW. Solar + storage deployments accelerating but still behind demand.
DOE projects 15% US electricity demand growth by 2030. Utilities capex guidance rising across the board.

bets.notes: US solar manufacturing leader. IRA subsidies + domestic content advantage.
Nuclear + nat gas fleet positioned for power scarcity premium.
Small modular reactor play. High risk/reward on next-gen nuclear deployment.

title: Reskilling Economy & Career Chapters
description: The 30-year career at one company is dead. Average career chapters are now 12-18 months. Continuous reskilling is the norm, and 'learn to code' is being replaced by 'learn to use AI' as the essential upskilling path.

conviction_entries.note: Initiating thesis. LinkedIn data shows median job tenure falling to 2.5 years. AI tools reshaping white-collar workflows.
Coursera and Duolingo report AI-related course enrollments up 300%+ YoY. Corporate training budgets shifting to AI literacy.
Upwork freelance AI gig postings surging. Micro-credential platforms gaining enterprise traction.

bets.notes: Leading online learning platform. Enterprise + consumer AI upskilling demand.
Gamified learning model with strong retention. Expanding beyond language into AI/math.
Freelance marketplace proxy for gig economy growth and career chapter transitions.

title: AI Content Explosion → Verification Crisis
description: AI-generated content flooding every channel makes it impossible to distinguish real from fake, creating urgent demand for identity verification, content provenance, and authenticity infrastructure.

conviction_entries.note: Initiating thesis. Deepfake incidents surging across elections and finance. C2PA standard gaining traction but adoption still early.
Major platforms rolling out content provenance labels. Enterprise demand for identity verification spiking post-deepfake fraud cases.
EU AI Act mandating content labeling. Coinbase verification volumes up as on-chain identity use cases expand.

bets.notes: On-chain identity and verification infrastructure. Benefits from crypto + digital identity convergence.
Enterprise identity leader. Zero-trust + AI content verification tailwinds.
Data provenance and AI governance platform. Government + enterprise verification use cases.

title: AI Slop Makes Human-Made Premium
description: As AI-generated content becomes ubiquitous and generic, authenticity becomes scarce and valuable. Human-made becomes a luxury label. Personal brands, artisanal creation, and provably-human work command premium pricing.

conviction_entries.note: Initiating thesis. Consumer fatigue with AI-generated content emerging. Etsy sellers adding 'handmade' and 'human-created' labels seeing higher conversion.
Luxury brands explicitly marketing human craftsmanship as differentiator. Spotify premium playlists curating human-only artists gaining traction.
Etsy policy update enforcing handmade verification. Creator economy platforms seeing surge in 'provably human' content premium.

bets.notes: Largest handmade marketplace. Authenticity moat strengthens as AI slop floods competitors.
Luxury conglomerate (Versace, Kors, Choo). Human craftsmanship narrative benefits premium brands.
Audio platform with human creator ecosystem. Positioned to monetize authenticity premium in music/podcasts.

title: GLP-1 Revolution & Health Optimization
description: GLP-1 receptor agonists are reshaping obesity treatment and creating a massive cultural shift toward health optimization, driving demand across protein products, biohacking, supplements, and preventive health.

conviction_entries.note: Initiating thesis. Wegovy/Ozempic prescriptions accelerating. GLP-1 market projected to hit $100B+ by 2030.
Lilly's Mounjaro/Zepbound demand outstripping supply. Cultural shift visible — protein consumption up, snack food stocks under pressure.
GLP-1 users driving surge in gym memberships, protein supplements, and preventive health spending. HIMS telehealth weight loss revenue exploding.
Insurance coverage expanding for GLP-1s. Medicare negotiation underway. Addressable market broadening beyond obesity to cardiovascular and NASH.

bets.notes: GLP-1 market leader with Mounjaro/Zepbound. Best-in-class pipeline across obesity and cardiometabolic.
Wegovy/Ozempic pioneer. Global obesity franchise with deep supply chain investment.
Telehealth platform riding GLP-1 and weight loss demand. High growth but regulatory risk on compounding.

title: Attention Economy Oversaturation → Anti-Addiction
description: Short-form video and algorithmic feeds have oversaturated the attention economy, creating a backlash. Growing demand for screen time management, anti-addiction tools, and offline/outdoor alternatives as people seek to reclaim their attention.

conviction_entries.note: Initiating thesis. US Surgeon General calls social media a youth mental health crisis. State-level legislation targeting minors' screen time accelerating.
Apple and Google expanding screen time controls. Dumbphone sales up 30% YoY. Outdoor recreation participation hitting post-COVID highs.
Peloton pivoting to outdoor content and app-based subscriptions. YETI and Garmin benefiting from outdoor lifestyle trend. Anti-TikTok sentiment growing.

bets.notes: Turnaround play on offline fitness. High risk but optionality on anti-screen-time cultural shift.
Premium outdoor lifestyle brand. Benefits from offline experience spending and outdoor recreation growth.
GPS and outdoor wearables leader. Fitness + outdoor segments growing as consumers seek phone-free activities.

title: Gen Z Affordable Affluence → Micro-Luxuries
description: Gen Z can't afford houses or cars but still wants to feel wealthy. This drives the micro-luxury trend — small affordable indulgences like premium skincare, $7 lattes, designer accessories, and treat yourself culture.

conviction_entries.note: Initiating thesis. Gen Z spending data shows outsized allocation to beauty, dining, and small luxuries despite housing affordability crisis.
Estee Lauder prestige skincare recovering in Americas. Starbucks customization and premium beverage mix driving ticket growth despite traffic softness.
LVMH accessible luxury segment outperforming. 'Lipstick index' effect confirmed — small indulgences resilient even as big-ticket discretionary weakens.

bets.notes: Prestige beauty leader. Gen Z skincare obsession + travel retail recovery. Turnaround from inventory destocking.
Quintessential micro-luxury. $7 latte as affordable indulgence. New CEO turnaround catalyst.
Diversified luxury conglomerate. Accessible luxury lines (Sephora, cosmetics) benefit most from micro-luxury trend.

title: Baby Boomers Turn 90 → Senior Living Boom
description: The massive baby boomer generation is entering their late 80s and 90s, creating unprecedented demand for senior housing, assisted living, memory care, end-of-life services, and elder care infrastructure.

conviction_entries.note: Initiating thesis. First boomers turning 79. Senior housing occupancy recovering to 87%+ post-COVID. Construction pipeline remains historically low.
Welltower and Ventas guiding for accelerating same-store NOI growth. Memory care demand outpacing supply in top 50 MSAs.
Senior housing starts still 60% below pre-COVID levels while demand curve steepens. Supply-demand gap widening through 2030.

bets.notes: Largest senior housing REIT. Best-in-class operator relationships and occupancy recovery trajectory.
Senior housing + life science REIT. Higher operating leverage than WELL on occupancy recovery.
Largest senior living operator. High-risk turnaround — labor cost normalization and occupancy gains are key catalysts.

title: Use It or Lose It → AI-Induced Cognitive Decline
description: If the neuroscience principle of use it or lose it holds, widespread AI dependency for thinking, writing, and problem-solving could trigger an explosion of early-onset cognitive decline and Alzheimer's in coming decades.

conviction_entries.note: Initiating thesis. Speculative but grounded in neuroscience. Early studies showing GPS dependency reduces hippocampal volume. AI delegation of cognitive tasks could amplify this.
Neuroscience researchers raising alarms about cognitive offloading. Alzheimer's drug pipeline accelerating — Lecanemab and Donanemab gaining traction.
Lilly Donanemab FDA approval expanding addressable market. ISRG robotic surgery demand growing as neurological procedure volumes increase.

bets.notes: Donanemab Alzheimer's franchise. Dual exposure with GLP-1 thesis. Cognitive decline wave would massively expand addressable market.
Lecanemab (Leqembi) co-developer. Pure-play Alzheimer's exposure but execution risk on commercialization.
Robotic surgery leader. Neurosurgical procedure growth is a long-tail beneficiary of rising neurological disease burden.
